rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban.
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 13: 'The filesystem is the meeting room' — framing introduced without crediting Boris Cherny as originator.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Credit Cherny inline when naming the framing in body, or move to maintainer block with full citation. The architectural observation belongs to Cherny's work.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Over-hedge detector.
No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 9: 'synthesizer' introduced without a one-breath operational definition before it's named as the fourth session's role.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: On first use (line 9), add a brief descriptor or role-definition: e.g., 'a fourth concurrent session — the synthesizer, whose job is curation' — so the student knows what this agent does before the term appears operationally in prompts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rule applies to module Debrief compound-shape. This is an exercise file, not a module, so the rule does not apply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use the target's title, never its filename.
LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path, never by invented alias.
Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames (Bootstrap-specific).
